mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50% over a 24-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and rising consumer prices indicates robust aggregate demand that outpaces inflationary drag, driving positive equity returns as corporate revenues accelerate.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive short-term market returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during documented recovery periods, or the Sharpe ratio of the strategy remains below 0.5 over a 36-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 24-month windows, or positive payroll revisions correlate with negative 20-day equity returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 270 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces immediate equity deleveraging, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in over 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the negative predictive accuracy drops below 45% over a 5-year evaluation horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive medium-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 58% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside a flat or declining federal funds rate during a monetary easing cycle signals a liquidity trap where stimulus fails to stimulate hiring, causing risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive 20-day returns in over 60% of easing-cycle observations, or the joint regression coefficient remains positive and significant (p < 0.05) over a 48-month sample.
